rulings.Case Title: VIOLET FONG HOM VS. CYNTHIA JONE FONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Ntc Of Mtn By Defts Cynthia Fong And Genevieve Ng For Attorney Fees And Costs; Memo Of Pts And Authorities; Declaration Of James Cai, Esq.
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 29, 2014, line 1. DEFENDANTS CYNTHIA FONG and GENEVIEVE NG's Amended Motion For Attorney Fees And Costs DENIED without prejudice. Moving party fails to provide sufficient evidence to support its claim that attorney's services described in Exhibit B to Cai's declaration were for the "common benefit" of the parties pursuant to CCP 874.010. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: GSQUARED RESTAURANT VENTURES, LLC VS. COCOA DEVELOPMENT RETAIL, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Sanctions Against Pltf Gsquared Restaurant Ventures, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 29, 2014, line 3. DEFENDANT GHIRARDELLI ACQUISITION COMPANY's Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Gsquared Restaurant Ventures, LLC Continued to June 30, 2014. Gary Danko shall appear for additional deposition so that Defendant may explore his assertions regarding facts forming the basis of responses to Form Interrogatories. Rifkind v. Sup. Ct (1994) 22 al.App.4th 1255. Deposition shall take place at a mutually convenient time no later than June 18, 2014. Plaintiff shall pay costs and reasonable attorney's fees incurred for the deposition. Defendant shall file and serve a declaration no later than June 24, 2014 regarding costs and fees and supplemental brief (not to exceed 5 pages), if any, regarding the outcome of the deposition. Plaintiff may file a response (not to exceed 5 pages), if any, no later than June 26, 2014. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOE TABONI et al VS. VERMONT STREET TOWN HOMES LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION (Joinder In Deft Tim Brown'S) Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 29, 2014, line 4. DEFENDANT ANGUS MCCARTHY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION (Joinder In Deft Tim Brown's) Of Issues DENIED. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOE TABONI et al VS. VERMONT STREET TOWN HOMES LLC, et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 29, 2014, line 4. DEFENDANT TIMOTHY BROWN's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350 (b), i.e. failed to state the issues to be adjudicated in the notice of motion and repeat them, verbatim, in the separate statement of undisputed material facts. Specifically, (1) Notice of Motion lists the issues to be summarily adjudicated as "the First Cause of Action (for specific performance), the Second Cause of action (for breach of contract) ¿, on the basis that defendant is entitled to judgment on such causes of action because no enforcement contract was formed with plaintiffs Toboni for the sale of the real property which is the subject of the alleged contract, or signed by Sullivan or an authorized agent;" and (2) Separate Statement lists the issues to be adjudicated as, for example "ISSUES No. 1: Defendant is entitled to judgment on the first cause of action for specific performance because the contract alleged is not executed by seller Vermont Street Town Homes or a duly authorized agent." = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOE TABONI et al VS. VERMONT STREET TOWN HOMES LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues And Exhibit Appendix
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 29, 2014, line 4. DEFENDANT ANGUS MCCARTHY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350 (b), i.e. failed to state the issues to be adjudicated in the notice of motion and repeat them, verbatim, in the separate statement of undisputed material facts. Specifically, (1) Notice of Motion lists the issues to be summarily adjudicated as "the Eighth Cause of action (for declaratory relief as to contract) on the basis that defendant Angus McCarthy is entitled to judgment on such causes [sic] of action because no enforcement [sic] contract was formed with plaintiffs Toboni for the sale of the real property which is the subject of the alleged contract, or signed by Sullivan or an authorizing [sic] agent. In addition, Plaintiffs cannot meet the elements required by specific performance. Therefore, if Plaintiffs are entitled to any relief under their cause of action for declaratory relief, it is for damages from the other defendants, not Angus McCarthy;" and and (2) Separate Statement lists the issue to be adjudicated as "ISSUE No. 1: Defendant is entitled to judgment on the eighth cause of action for declaratory relief as to the duties arising out of such contract because _____ ." = (501/REQ)
